artifact.artifact_title: Scheduler Migration Note: Evidence Sufficiency for Artifact Non-Inferiority Experiments

claims.text: SchedulerClock is established as the deterministic semantic baseline for scheduler experiments.
No production scheduler components (daemon, sleep loop, wall-clock SLA, process supervisor) were built.
Paired DES and wall-clock smoke tests passed for due ordering, expiration, next-wake reporting, and restart frontier recovery.

artifact.conclusion: The scheduler evidence (deterministic semantic baseline, wall-clock smoke test pass, and absence of a production scheduler) is sufficient to proceed with artifact non-inferiority experiments.
declared_losses: Production scheduler was not built, so production non-inferiority cannot be directly assessed; this note only covers artifact non-inferiority.
Production scheduler was not built, so production non-inferiority cannot be directly assessed; this note only covers artifact non-inferiority.

messages.content: Return only one JSON object. Required fields: artifact_title string, task_id string, conclusion string, recommended_action string, claims array, declared_losses array, cited_record_ids array. Each claim must have fact_id, text, status, and evidence_record_ids. Use fact_id values from this set only: ['des_baseline', 'production_scheduler_absent', 'wall_clock_probe_passed'].

content.prompt: Write a migration note deciding whether the scheduler evidence is sufficient to continue to artifact non-inferiority experiments.

content.text: Goal 9 kept SchedulerClock as the deterministic semantic baseline for scheduler experiments.
Goal 9 paired DES and wall-clock smoke tests passed for due ordering, expiration, next-wake reporting, and restart frontier recovery.
Goal 9 did not build a daemon, sleep loop, wall-clock SLA, or production process supervisor.
Unrelated blog note: use a fairy-tale cellar metaphor for the memory system.
